spec_match.rationale: The filter comparison accurately shows the distinct visual difference in filter application on same-origin vs cross-origin iframes.

interactive.rationale: The user can interactively toggle the filter class on and off for both cards to witness the difference.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature presentation matches the index description for a side-by-side visual comparison.

edge_cases.rationale: It demonstrates the iframe case successfully but does not cover embedded plugins (PDFs) which are also affected by this change.

references.rationale: The chromestatus.com feature reference link is present and correctly cited.

design_system.rationale: It leverages CSS variables, clean utility classes, and standard typography from the styles design system.

openQuestions.title: Include a PDF or object plugin test case
openQuestions.detail: The SVG filter restriction also applies to embedded plugins (like PDFs). Add a third comparison panel with an embedded PDF plugin (using <embed> or <object>) to demonstrate that it is also unfiltered in Chrome 150+.

openQuestions.title: Ensure explicit text contrast on pressed buttons
openQuestions.detail: When the Filter ON button is pressed, it gets the background color var(--accent-blue). Ensure the text color (color property) is set explicitly to maintain high contrast (WCAG AA) over the blue background.

summary: The filter-comparison concept provides a quick, interactive way to toggle and observe how Chrome 150+ allows SVG filters on same-origin content but strips them from cross-origin iframes.
